exchange_a.name: dYdX
strengths: Established DEX brand with proven track record
Cosmos appchain sovereignty for better performance
weaknesses: Migration complexity from StarkEx to Cosmos
Limited trading pair selection compared to CEXs

exchange_b.name: Bluefin
strengths: Leading perpetual DEX in Sui ecosystem
Professional trading UI with institutional features
Sub-second settlement times on Sui blockchain
weaknesses: Sui ecosystem still in early adoption phase
Low overall trading volume affects rate stability

rate_mechanics: Both dYdX and Bluefin use 1-hour funding intervals with similar rate calculation methodologies based on premium/discount to spot prices. dYdX benefits from higher volume leading to more stable rate patterns, while Bluefin's lower liquidity can create more volatile funding rates. Both platforms use TWAP-based calculations but dYdX's mature orderbook typically produces more predictable rate movements. Settlement speed favors Bluefin due to Sui's sub-second finality versus Cosmos block times.
verdict: dYdX remains the more established choice for funding rate arbitrage with higher liquidity and proven rate stability, despite post-migration volume challenges. Bluefin offers superior settlement speeds and a clean trading interface but lacks the volume depth needed for consistent arbitrage opportunities.

faq.question: Which platform offers better funding rate arbitrage opportunities?
faq.answer: dYdX typically provides more consistent arbitrage opportunities due to higher trading volumes and more predictable rate patterns, though both use identical 1-hour funding intervals.

faq.question: How do settlement speeds compare between dYdX and Bluefin?
faq.answer: Bluefin offers faster settlement with sub-second finality on Sui, while dYdX operates on Cosmos with slightly longer block times but still maintains efficient execution.

faq.question: Are trading fees identical on both platforms?
faq.answer: Yes, both platforms use the same fee structure of 0.02% for makers and 0.05% for takers, making fee costs neutral in platform selection for arbitrage.

faq.question: Which DEX has better liquidity for large arbitrage positions?
faq.answer: dYdX maintains deeper liquidity pools despite post-migration volume decline, while Bluefin's emerging status in the Sui ecosystem means smaller position sizes are more suitable currently.
